title: Naval: How to Get Rich - Part 1: Introduction & Wealth Definition

segments.en: Hey this is Nivi you're listening to the Naval podcast. This is one giant mega episode that collects every episode we've done on getting rich. All of it based on his tweet storm of 'How to get rich without getting lucky.' I've collected them all here because we're going to switch topics to the new topic of happiness on the next episode.
segments.cn: 大家好，我是Nivi，欢迎你收听Naval播客。这是一个超级合集，汇集了我们所有关于致富的节目。全部基于他的推文风暴《如何不靠运气致富》。我把它们都收集在这里，因为在下一集我们要切换到幸福这个新主题。

segments.en: We've published one of these giant mega episodes before but this one's even bigger. It's about three and a half hours long. It covers all the tweets from the 'How to get rich' tweet storm plus all the Q&A that we did after that, plus ten minutes of bonus material at the very end that we've never released. The overall sound quality of this mega episode improves a lot after the first hour.
segments.cn: 我们之前发布过一个这样的超级合集，但这个更大。大约有三个半小时长。它涵盖了「如何致富」推文风暴的所有推文，加上之后我们做的所有问答，再加上最后十分钟我们从未发布的额外内容。这个超级合集的整体音质在第一小时后有了很大改善。

segments.en: You probably know Naval from his Twitter account and we're gonna be talking about his epic tweet storm on how to get rich without getting lucky. We're going to go through most of the tweets in detail, giving Naval a chance to expand on them and just generally riff on the topic. He'll probably throw in some ideas that he hasn't even published before.
segments.cn: 你可能从Twitter账号知道Naval，我们要讨论他关于如何不靠运气致富的史诗级推文风暴。我们将详细讲解大部分推文，让Naval有机会对它们进行扩展，并总体上对这个话题进行即兴发挥。他可能会加入一些他以前从未发表过的想法。

segments.en: He's also the co-founder of AngelList and Epinions. He's a prolific tech investor in companies like Twitter, Uber, and many more. And I'm the co-founder evangelist of Naval. And I also co-authored the Venture Hacks blog with him back in the day.
segments.cn: 他也是AngelList和Epinions的联合创始人。他是一位多产的科技投资者，投资了Twitter、Uber等许多公司。而我是Naval的联合创始人兼布道者。早年我还和他一起撰写了Venture Hacks博客。

segments.en: This tweet storm definitely hit a nerve. A lot of people say it was helpful. It reached across aisles and people outside of tech industry, people in all walks of life. People do want to know how to solve their money problems. And everyone vaguely knows that they want to be wealthy, but they don't have a good set of principles to do it by.
segments.cn: 这个推文风暴确实触动了神经。很多人说它很有帮助。它跨越了不同领域和科技行业之外的人，各行各业的人。人们确实想知道如何解决他们的金钱问题。每个人都模糊地知道他们想要变得富有，但他们没有一套好的原则来做到这一点。

segments.en: What's the difference between wealth, money, and status? Wealth is the thing that you really want. Wealth is assets that earn while you sleep. Wealth is the factory with the robots cranking out things. Wealth is the computer program that's running at night that's serving other customers. Wealth is even money in the bank that is being reinvested into other assets and into other businesses.
segments.cn: 财富、金钱和地位之间有什么区别？财富是你真正想要的东西。财富是睡觉时仍在为你赚钱的资产。财富是工厂里机器人正在生产东西。财富是夜晚仍在运行、为其他客户服务的计算机程序。财富甚至是银行里被重新投资到其他资产和业务中的资金。

segments.en: Even a house can be a form of wealth because you can rent it out, although it's probably a lower use of productivity. But land and actually doing some commercial enterprise. So my definition of wealth is much more businesses and assets that can earn while you sleep.
segments.cn: 甚至房子也可以是一种财富形式，因为你可以出租它，尽管这可能是生产力的较低用途。但土地和实际从事商业企业。所以我对财富的定义更多是那些可以在你睡觉时赚钱的企业和资产。

segments.en: But really the reason you want wealth is because it buys you freedom. So you don't have to wear a tie like a collar around your neck. So you don't have to wake up at 7 a.m. and rush to work and sit in commute traffic. So you don't have to waste away your entire life grinding all the productive hours into a soulless job that doesn't fulfill you.
segments.cn: 但你真正想要财富的原因是它能买到自由。这样你就不用像被项圈拴住一样系着领带。不用早上7点起床，匆忙赶去上班，在通勤交通中煎熬。不用把你生命中所有富有生产力的时光都消磨在一份无法给你带来满足感的无灵魂的工作中。

segments.en: So the purpose of wealth is freedom. It's nothing more than that. It's not to buy fur coats or drive Ferraris or sail yachts or jet around the world in your Gulfstream. That stuff gets really boring and really stupid really fast.
segments.cn: 所以财富的目的是自由，仅此而已。不是为了买皮草大衣或开法拉利或驾驶游艇或乘坐湾流飞机环游世界。那些东西很快就会变得无聊和愚蠢。

segments.en: It's really just so that you are your own sovereign individual. You're not gonna get that unless you really want it. And the entire world wants it. The entire world is working hard at it. To some extent it is competitive. It's a positive sum game, but there are competitive elements to it because there's a finite amount of resources right now in society.
segments.cn: 它真的只是为了让你成为自己的主权个体。除非你真正想要，否则你不会得到它。而整个世界都想要它。整个世界都在为此努力工作。在某种程度上这是竞争性的。这是一个正和游戏，但其中存在竞争元素，因为社会中当前的资源是有限的。

segments.en: To get the resources to do what you want, you have to stand out. Money is how we transfer wealth. Money is social credits. It is the ability to have credits and debits on other people's time.
segments.cn: 要获得资源去做你想做的事，你必须脱颖而出。金钱是我们转移财富的方式。金钱是社会信用。它是在他人时间上拥有债权和债务的能力。

segments.en: If I do my job right, if I create value for society, society says 'oh thank you, we owe you something in the future for the work that you did in the past.' Here's a little IOU. Let's call that money. And that money gets debased because people steal the IOUs, the government prints extra IOUs.
segments.cn: 如果我做好了我的工作，如果我为社会创造了价值，社会会说「哦谢谢你，我们欠你一些东西，作为你过去工作的回报」。这是一张小小的欠条。我们称之为金钱。而金钱会被贬值，因为人们偷欠条，政府印额外的欠条。

segments.en: But really what money is trying to be, it is trying to be a reliable IOU from society. You are owed something for something you or someone who gave you that money did in the past. We can transfer these IOUs around. So really money is how we transfer wealth.
segments.cn: 但金钱真正想要成为的，是试图成为来自社会的可靠欠条。你或给你钱的人在过去做了某些事情，因此你应得某些东西。我们可以传递这些欠条。所以金钱实际上是我们转移财富的方式。

segments.en: There are fundamentally two huge games in life that people play. One is the money game. Because money's not gonna solve all your problems, but it's gonna solve all your money problems. I think that people know that, they realize that, so they want to make money.
segments.cn: 人们在生活中玩的基本上有两个巨大的游戏。一个是金钱游戏。因为金钱不会解决你所有的问题，但它会解决你所有的金钱问题。我认为人们知道这一点，他们意识到这一点，所以他们想赚钱。

segments.en: But at the same time, many of them deep down believe that they can't make it. They don't want any wealth creation to happen. So they virtue signal by attacking the whole enterprise, by saying 'well, making money is evil, capitalism is evil.' But what they're trying to do is they're actually playing the other game, the status game.
segments.cn: 但与此同时，他们中的许多人内心深处相信他们做不到。他们不想要任何财富创造发生。所以他们通过攻击整个企业来展示美德，说「赚钱是邪恶的，资本主义是邪恶的」。但他们试图做的是他们实际上在玩另一个游戏，地位游戏。

segments.en: They're trying to be high status in the eyes of other people watching by saying, 'oh, I don't need money, we don't want money.' And then status is just your ranking in the social hierarchy.
segments.cn: 他们试图在看他们的其他人眼中获得高地位，说「哦，我不需要钱，我们不想要钱」。然后地位只是你在社会等级中的排名。

segments.en: So wealth is not a zero-sum game. Everybody in the world can have a house. Because you have a house doesn't take away from my ability to have a house. If anything, the more houses that are built, the easier it becomes to build houses, the more we know about building houses, and just the more people that can have houses.
segments.cn: 所以财富不是零和游戏。世界上每个人都可以拥有一所房子。因为你有一所房子不会减少我拥有房子的能力。如果有的话，建造的房子越多，建造房子变得越容易，我们对建造房子了解越多，就有越多的人可以拥有房子。

segments.en: So wealth is a very positive-sum game. We're starting this endeavor to create this hopefully piece of art that explains what we're doing. At the end of it, something brand-new will be created. It's a positive-sum game.
segments.cn: 所以财富是一个非常正和的游戏。我们开始这个努力来创造这件希望是艺术的作品，解释我们在做什么。最后，一些全新的东西将被创造出来。这是一个正和游戏。

segments.en: Status, on the other hand, is a zero-sum game. It's a very old game. We've been playing it since monkey tribes, and it's hierarchical. Who's number one, who's number two, who's number three. And for number three to move to number two, number two has to move out of that slot.
segments.cn: 另一方面，地位是一个零和游戏。这是一个非常古老的游戏。自从猴子部落以来我们一直在玩它，它是等级制的。谁是第一，谁是第二，谁是第三。对于第三名移动到第二名，第二名必须离开那个位置。

segments.en: So status is a zero-sum game. Politics is an example of a status game. Even sports is an example of a status game. To be the winner, there must be a loser. I don't fundamentally love status games. They play an important role in our society so we figure out who's in charge, but fundamentally you play them because they're a necessary evil.
segments.cn: 所以地位是一个零和游戏。政治是地位游戏的一个例子。甚至体育也是地位游戏的一个例子。要成为赢家，必须有输家。我并不从根本上喜欢地位游戏。它们在我们的社会中发挥着重要作用，这样我们才能弄清楚谁是负责人，但从根本上说你玩它们是因为它们是必要的恶。

segments.en: The problem is on an evolutionary basis, if you go back thousands of years, status is a much better predictor of survival than wealth. You couldn't have wealth before the agricultural age. Before the agricultural age, you were a hunter-gatherer. You didn't store things up.
segments.cn: 问题在于在进化基础上，如果你回到几千年前，地位比财富更能预测生存。在农业时代之前你不可能拥有财富。在农业时代之前，你是狩猎采集者。你不会储存东西。

segments.title: 地位游戏者攻击财富创造者

segments.en: People playing the status game will always try to undermine people playing the wealth creation game. They're trying to be high status in the status game. They're going around saying, 'oh I don't need money, we don't want money.' What they're trying to do is they're trying to stop you from getting wealthy because they want to preserve their status.
segments.cn: 玩地位游戏的人总是会试图破坏玩财富创造游戏的人。他们试图在地位游戏中获得高地位。他们四处说：「哦，我不需要钱，我们不想要钱」。他们试图做的是阻止你变得富有，因为他们想保住自己的地位。

segments.en: If you really want to be wealthy, you have to be internally okay with creating wealth. Understand that ethical wealth creation is possible. If you secretly despise wealth, it will elude you. If you secretly believe that making money is evil or bad, then it will elude you.
segments.cn: 如果你真的想变得富有，你必须在内心对创造财富感到自在。理解道德财富创造是可能的。如果你内心鄙视财富，它就会躲避你。如果你内心深处相信赚钱是邪恶或坏的，那么它就会躲避你。

segments.en: You have to be internally okay with it. You have to be internally convinced that you can do it, and it is a good thing, and you should do it, and you're going to do it ethically. Ignore people playing status games. They gain status by attacking people playing wealth creation games.
segments.cn: 你必须在内心对它感到自在。你必须在内心确信你能做到，这是一件好事，你应该做，并且你要以道德的方式去做。忽视那些玩地位游戏的人。他们通过攻击玩财富创造游戏的人来获得地位。

segments.en: They're just trying to make you feel bad so they can feel better about themselves. You're not going to get rich renting out your time. You must own equity, a piece of a business, to gain your financial freedom.
segments.cn: 他们只是试图让你感觉不好，这样他们就能对自己感觉更好。你不会通过出租自己的时间变得富有。你必须拥有股权，企业的一部分，才能获得财务自由。

segments.en: You will get rich by giving society what it wants, but does not yet know how to get, at scale. Pick an industry where you can play long-term games with long-term people. The Internet has massively broadened the possible space of careers. Most people haven't figured this out yet.
segments.cn: 你会通过规模化地给予社会它想要但还不知道如何获得的东西而变得富有。挑选一个你可以与长期主义者玩长期游戏的行业。互联网极大地拓宽了职业的可能空间。大多数人还没有意识到这一点。

segments.en: Play iterated games. All the returns in life, whether in wealth, relationships, or knowledge, come from compound interest. Pick business partners with high intelligence, high energy, and above all, high integrity.
segments.cn: 玩迭代游戏。生活中的所有回报，无论是财富、关系还是知识，都来自复利。选择具有高智商、高能量和高诚信的商业伙伴。

segments.en: Don't partner with cynics and pessimists. Their beliefs are self-fulfilling. Learn to sell. Learn to build. If you can do both, you will be unstoppable.
segments.cn: 不要与愤世嫉俗者和悲观者合作。他们的信仰是自我实现的。学习销售。学习建造。如果你两者都能做到，你将不可阻挡。
